title_en: How South Korean Buddhists are trying to stay relevant
title_zh: 韩国佛教徒如何让自己保持相关性
rubric_en: Life is suffering; the Buddha can help
rubric_zh: 人生苦难；佛陀可以提供帮助

paragraphs.en: A ROBOT MONK trundles across the exhibition hall of the Korean Buddhism Culture Expo in the city of Daegu. The humanoid, called Gabi, was unveiled in May and has since generated a new buzz about an ancient religion. Buddhists in South Korea have recently been trying to appear less old-fashioned, says Bae Ji-yun, a 26-year-old attending the expo and sifting through T-shirts bearing slogans such as “Shut up and meditate”. Their efforts seem to be paying off. The event used to draw mainly Buddhists, but it increasingly attracts casual shoppers too. A similar expo this year in Seoul, the capital, drew a quarter of a million visitors, mostly youngsters.
paragraphs.zh: 在大邱市的韩国佛教文化展览会展厅里，一位“机器人和尚”正缓慢地穿行。这个名叫 Gabi 的类人机器人在今年5月首次亮相，此后为一项古老的宗教带来了新的讨论热潮。26岁的白智允（Bae Ji-yun），她在展会上来回挑选印有“Shut up and meditate（闭嘴去冥想）”等口号的T恤时说，韩国佛教徒最近一直在努力让自己显得不那么老派。她们的努力似乎正在奏效。这个活动过去主要吸引佛教徒，如今越来越多的是普通逛街人群。今年在首尔（首都）举行的类似展会吸引了25万人次，多为年轻人。

paragraphs.en: Gloomy young Koreans are drawn to Buddhism. Young adults report that life is increasingly stressful and competitive. The number of young who are economically inactive is said to have nearly tripled since 2004. Buddhism, which views suffering as an unavoidable feature of life and strives to rise above it, offers them some comfort. The religion generates faith in oneself rather than in a deity, says Kim Su-jin, a 25-year-old who recently trekked to a temple in search of inner peace. Buddhism remains the religion viewed most favourably by secular Koreans, a recent poll shows. Books about mindfulness such as “Life Hacks from the Buddha” are bestsellers. Last year temples hosted a record number of overnight guests.
paragraphs.zh: 郁闷的韩国年轻人正被佛教吸引。许多年轻成年人表示，生活越来越有压力且充满竞争。自2004年以来，青年“非经济活跃”人数据说几乎翻了三倍。佛教认为痛苦是人生中不可避免的特征，并努力超越痛苦，这给他们提供了一些安慰。25岁的金秀真（Kim Su-jin）表示，佛教带来的信仰重心在于对自我而非对神明的信仰。她最近为了寻求内心平静，曾徒步前往一座寺庙。最近的一项民意调查显示，佛教仍然是世俗韩国人最认可的宗教。像《佛陀的人生黑科技》（Life Hacks from the Buddha）这样的正念读物正在畅销。去年，寺庙接待的过夜住客人数创下了新高。

paragraphs.en: Buddhist leaders hope the interest will revitalise their fading religion. South Koreans, especially young ones, are becoming less religious overall. The number of practising Buddhists is shrinking. Fewer new monks join monasteries: ordinations within the Jogye order, the largest in South Korea, have dropped to less than a third of their level two decades ago.
paragraphs.zh: 佛教领导层希望这种兴趣能让他们式微的宗教复兴。韩国人，尤其是年轻人，整体上越来越不那么虔诚。实际的信徒人数在减少。越来越少的新僧人加入寺院：韩国最大的曹溪宗（Jogye order）中的出家人数已降至二十年前的不到三分之一。

paragraphs.en: Last year, the Association of Buddhist Orders in Korea convened to discuss Buddhism’s survival in the country. The latest challenge is to arouse lasting engagement while remaining uncoercive—one of Buddhism’s key selling points.
paragraphs.zh: 去年，韩国佛教宗团联合会召开会议，讨论佛教在韩国的生存问题。当前的挑战是，在保持非强制性这一佛教核心吸引力的同时，激发持久参与。

paragraphs.en: To appeal to younger people, Buddhist orders are these days throwing dance parties and meditation festivals. Some temples even host matchmaking events for singles. These aren’t gimmicks, says the Venerable Seo One of the Jikjisa Temple, one of the Koreas’ oldest, dating back to the fifth century. Buddhism, she says, has always embraced “an unorthodox culture that transcends class and age”. Others fear the religion is being cherry-picked. “The core beliefs may get lost,” worries the Venerable Il Hwa of the Cultural Corps of Korean Buddhism.
paragraphs.zh: 为了吸引年轻人，许多佛教道场如今会举办舞会和冥想节。甚至有些寺院还会为单身男女举办相亲活动。智积寺的一位法师 Seo 表示，这些都不是噱头。智积寺是韩国最古老的寺院之一，始于公元5世纪。她说，佛教历来就包容一种“超越阶层和年龄的非传统文化”。也有人担心，佛教正在被“挑挑拣拣”地消费：“核心教义可能会被遗忘，”韩国佛教文化会的 Il Hwa 法师担忧道。

paragraphs.en: It may be so. Hipster brands sell merch and preach the importance of inner wellbeing. One brand, Haetal Company, prints Buddhism-inspired memes on mugs and plastic key-chains. “I have always found Buddhism incredibly fun and cool,” says Ju Yeo-jin, its founder. The knick-knacks may not be a path to true enlightenment, but at least they are lifting spirits. ■
paragraphs.zh: 这可能属实。新潮品牌会卖周边并强调内在福祉的重要性。一个名为 Haetal Company 的品牌就在杯子和塑料钥匙扣上印制受佛教启发的梗图。“我一直觉得佛教非常有趣、很酷，”该品牌创始人朱世镇（Ju Yeo-jin）说。那些小摆件可能不是通往真正开悟的道路，但至少能让人振奋精神。■

paragraphs.en: For exclusive coverage of Asian politics, economics and security, sign up to Asia Bulletin, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如需获取亚洲政治、经济与安全的独家报道，请订阅我们的每周付费通讯《Asia Bulletin》。
